concerns.text: FR-001 and FR-002 mandate downloading 'snap-cornell/twitter-roberta-base-sentiment-dataset' and aligning to 'quarterly' frequency. Tasks T014/T015 implement 'monthly' frequency and switch to GDELT (per plan). While the plan justifies this as a correction, the tasks lack a traceable tag or explicit note linking this deviation to a formal spec amendment or the specific 'Methodological Correction' section in plan.md. The implementer cannot satisfy the literal FR-001/FR-002 text without this explicit linkage.

FR-013 and US-2 specify a conflict resolution rule for Johansen tests (prioritize Trace unless Max-Eigenvalue exceeds critical value by >10%). Task T025 implements this rule. However, the plan.md 'Methodological Corrections' section explicitly states it 'removed the arbitrary >10% rule'. This creates a direct contradiction: the task implements a rule the plan says was removed. There is no task covering the 'Trace-only' approach mandated by the plan, leaving the implementation path ambiguous.

FR-012 and SC-006 require sensitivity analysis for '[deferred]' masking proportions. Task T050 hardcodes 10%, 20%, 30%. While this provides concrete values, there is no task or note explicitly linking these hardcoded values to the '[deferred]' placeholders in the spec, nor is there a task to 'resolve' these deferred values in a config file or research.md before implementation. The traceability from the deferred requirement to the concrete implementation is missing.

FR-005 requires NBER recession shading sourced from the NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee. Task T036a specifies a URL 'https://www.nber.org/business-cycle-dating/dates.csv' which is likely invalid (NBER typically provides HTML or requires scraping/API). The task does not include a fallback mechanism or a verification step for this specific URL, risking failure to satisfy the 'sourced from NBER' requirement if the URL is unreachable.

Task T025 includes a complex logic block ('re-evaluate lag order... if Max-Eigenvalue exceeds critical value by >10%') that contradicts the plan's explicit removal of this rule. This is a stale logic artifact from an older spec version that was not cleaned up in the tasks, creating confusion for the implementer.
